title: andropogon intermedius var. punctatus
intro: Andropogon intermedius var. punctatus, also known as Dhaman grass or Bara dhaman, is a type of plant that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Andropogon intermedius var. punctatus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and provide relief from digestive issues, among others. Andropogon intermedius var. punctatus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
